debug_info.goal:
C : Type
inst✝¹ : Category.{0, 0} C
K : Type
inst✝ : DivisionRing K
M : CategoryTheory.Functor C (ModuleCat K)
D : @Dual.DirectSumDecomposition C inst✝¹ K inst✝ M
hmax : IsMin D
x y : PersistenceSubmodule M
hxy : Disjoint x y
hN : x ⊔ y ∈ D
hx : ¬x = ⊥
hy : ¬y = ⊥
B : (N : PersistenceSubmodule M) → N ∈ D → Set (PersistenceSubmodule M) := ⋯
⊢ ∀ (N : PersistenceSubmodule M) (hN : N ∈ D), ⊥ ∉ B N hN

failed_attempts:
{
          exfalso
          simp_all [hN]
          <;>
          aesop
        })
      · simp_all [h]
        <;>
          (try {
            have h₂ : N = sSup {N} := by simp
            simp_all [h₂]
          })
        <;>
        (try {
          exfalso
          simp_all [hN]
          <;>
          aesop
        })
    ) (by
      intro N hN
      by_cases h : N = x ⊔ y
      · simp_all [h]
        <;>
          (try {
            have h₂ : sSupIndep ({x, y} : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := by
              have h₃ : x ≠ y := by
                intro h₃
                subst h₃
                exfalso
                have h₄ : Disjoint x x := hxy
                have h₅ : ¬Disjoint x x := by
                  intro h₅
                  have h₆ : x = ⊥ := h₅
                  simp_all [h₆]
                exact h₅ h₄
              have h₄ : sSupIndep ({x, y} : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := by
                rw [sSupIndep_pair]
                <;>
                (try {
                  aesop
                })
                <;>
                (try {
                  simp_all [h₃, hxy]
                  <;>
                  aesop
                })
                <;>
                (try {
                  exfalso
                  simp_all [hxy]
                  <;>
                  aesop
                })
              exact h₄
            simp_all [h₂]
          })
        <;>
        (try {
          exfalso
          simp_all [hN]
          <;>
          aesop
        })
      · simp_all [h]
        <;>
          (try {
            have h₂ : sSupIndep ({N} : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := by
              simp [sSupIndep_singleton]
            simp_all [h₂]
          })
        <;>
        (try {
          exfalso
          simp_all [hN]
          <;>
          aesop
        })
    ) (by
      intro N hN
      by_cases h : N = x ⊔ y
      · simp_all [h]
        <;>
          (try {
            intro h₃
            simp_all [Set.mem_insert, Set.mem_singleton_iff]
            <;>
            aesop
          })
        <;>
        (try {
          exfalso
          simp_all [hN]
          <;>
          aesop
        })
      · simp_all [h]
        <;>
          (try {
            intro h₃
            simp_all [Set.mem_insert, Set.mem_singleton_iff]
            <;>
            aesop
          })
        <;>
        (try {
          exfalso
          simp_all [hN]
          <;>
          aesop
        })
    ) where
  <;>

    <;>

simp_all [le_antisymm_iff]) <;>
          (try simp_all [Set.mem_singleton_iff]) <;>
          (try simp_all [Set.mem_insert_iff]) <;>
          (try simp_all [Set.mem_singleton_iff]) <;>
          (try simp_all [Set.mem_insert_iff]) <;>
          (try simp_all [le_antisymm_iff]) <;>
          (try simp_all [hxy])
          <;>
          aesop
        rw [h₁]
        <;> simp_all
      · rw [if_neg h]
        have h₁ : N = sSup ({N} : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := by
          simp [iSup_singleton]
        rw [h₁]
        <;> simp_all
    )
    (by
      intro N hN
      by_cases h : N = x ⊔ y
      · rw [if_pos h]
        -- We need to show that the set {x, y} is sSupIndep
        -- Since x and y are disjoint, the set {x, y} is sSupIndep
        have h₁ : sSupIndep ({x, y} : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := by
          rw [sSupIndep_pair]
          simp_all [hxy]
          <;>
          (try simp_all [Set.mem_singleton_iff]) <;>
          (try simp_all [Set.mem_insert_iff]) <;>
          (try simp_all [le_antisymm_iff]) <;>
          (try simp_all [hxy]) <;>
          (try tauto)
          <;>
          aesop
        exact h₁
      · rw [if_neg h]
        -- We need to show that the set {N} is sSupIndep
        -- This is trivially true as the set contains only one element
        have h₁ : sSupIndep ({N} : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := by
          rw [sSupIndep_singleton]
        exact h₁
    )
    (by
      intro N hN
      by_cases h : N = x ⊔ y
      · rw [if_pos h]
        -- We need to show that ⊥ is not in the set {x, y}
        -- Since x and y are not ⊥, ⊥ is not in the set
        simp_all [hx, hy]
        <;>
        (try simp_all [Set.mem_singleton_iff]) <;>
        (try simp_all [Set.mem_insert_iff]) <;>
        (try simp_all [le_antisymm_iff]) <;>
        (try simp_all [hxy]) <;>
        (try tauto)
        <;>
        aesop
      · rw [if_neg h]
        -- We need to show that ⊥ is not in the set {N}
        -- Since N is in D and D does not contain ⊥, ⊥ is not in the set
        simp_all [Set.mem_singleton_iff]
        <;>
        (try simp_all [hx, hy]) <;>
        (try simp_all [hN]) <;>
        (try simp_all [D.not_bot_mem'])
        <;>
        aesop
    )
  <;>
  (try simp_all) <;>
  (try aesop)
